title: Lavender infusion for mild low mood
subtitle: A gentle nervine infusion for moments of low spirits
excerpt: A hot infusion of dried lavender flowers, taken for mild, situational low mood and emotional flatness. Lavender's nervine and mood-lifting properties are among the best-studied herbal actions. This is a supportive preparation for mild, temporary low mood, not a treatment for depression.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve, A Modern Herbal (1931), lavender entry, nervine, antidepressant, and restorative properties. EMA HMPC monograph on Lavandulae flos (2011), confirms traditional use for anxiety, restlessness, and sleep disturbance. Silexan (standardised lavender oil preparation) clinical trials, Kasper et al. (2014, 2015), anxiolytic and mood effects in clinical trials, though at higher concentration than a kitchen infusion.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dried lavender flowers keep 12 months in a sealed tin away from light. Brew each cup fresh.

glossaryTerms.definition: A herb that acts on the nervous system to ease tension, anxiety, and low mood. Lavender's nervine action is backed by more clinical evidence than most traditional nervine herbs.

content.text: A cup of lavender tea for a difficult day. Lavender is the herbal tradition's most studied

for emotional wellbeing, its linalool acts on GABA receptors and serotonin pathways in ways that support mood, and a series of clinical trials on a standardised lavender oil preparation (Silexan) have demonstrated anxiolytic and mood-stabilising effects at higher concentrations than a kitchen infusion delivers. The kitchen cup of lavender is a gentler, quieter preparation, appropriate for mild, situational low mood rather than clinical depression, the kind of emotional flatness that comes with a difficult stretch, accumulated stress, or seasonal change. It is also simply a pleasant, fragrant cup of tea, which has its own value.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

This preparation is for mild, situational, temporary low mood only, the kind that lifts with rest, conversation, and small pleasures. It is not a treatment for clinical depression, which is a serious medical condition requiring proper assessment and treatment.

See your doctor if: low mood has persisted for more than two weeks; you are finding it hard to work, maintain relationships, or carry out normal daily activities; you have lost interest in things you normally enjoy; you are experiencing thoughts of hopelessness, self-harm, or suicide. These are symptoms of depression that need medical attention.

Pregnancy: lavender infusion at normal tea strength is widely used in pregnancy and is not associated with adverse effects.

St John's Wort (not this preparation) is the herbal medicine with the strongest evidence for mild-to-moderate depression, but it interacts with many medications, see a herbalist before using it.

items.prepNote: about 1 teaspoon of dried flowers, lavender is fragrant and a little goes a long way
optional, lavender tea can be slightly bitter; honey softens this

content.text: Place {{dried-lavender-flowers}} of dried lavender flowers in the teapot or infuser. Pour {{water}} of just-off-boil water over and lid immediately. Steep for exactly 10 minutes, beyond that, the floral note tips towards a soap-like quality. Strain and drink slowly. The scent is at least as therapeutic as the cup itself; sit with the cup for a moment before drinking.

One cup once or twice daily during a period of low mood or emotional stress. Lavender at normal infusion strength is safe for daily long-term use. There are no significant safety concerns at one to two cups per day.
When NOT to use this remedy

See your doctor if low mood has persisted for more than two weeks, is accompanied by changes in sleep, appetite, or concentration, is affecting your ability to work or maintain relationships, or involves any thoughts of self-harm or hopelessness. The lavender cup is a preparation for mild, temporary low mood, not for depression, which needs proper medical care.
